cot_prefix: Okay, let's try to figure this out. So the question is about determining the possible times Richard could have gone to the bakery, right? Let me start by going through all the information given. First, Richard woke up at 8am. That's the starting point. Then there are several people who saw him doing different things at different times. Let me list them out: - Betty saw him reading at the library from 9am to 2pm. - David saw him fixing a computer at the electronic store from 2pm to 4pm. - Mary saw him taking photos near the Eiffel Tower from 4pm to 5pm. - Hannah saw him sitting on a rooftop from 5pm to 6pm. - Susan saw him driving to the water park from 6pm to 7pm. Also, the bakery was closed after 7pm, so he had to go before that. Now, the key here is to figure out when Richard was unaccounted for. Since each of these people saw him doing something specific during their respective time frames, those times are when he was definitely not at the bakery. So we need to look for any gaps between these activities where he might have had time to go to the bakery. Let me map out his schedule step by step: 1. 8am: Wakes up. So from 8am onwards, he's awake. But there's no mention of what he did between 8am and 9am. That's a possible window. 2. 9am to 2pm: At the library with Betty. So during this time, he's occupied, so he can't go to the bakery then. That rules out option B (9am to 2pm). 3. 2pm to 4pm: At the electronic store with David. So again, occupied. So between 2pm and 4pm, he's not available. So any time during that window is out. 4. 4pm to 5pm: Taking photos with Mary. So again, occupied. So 4pm to 5pm is ruled out. 5. 5pm to 6pm: On the rooftop with Hannah. So that's another occupied time. So option C (5pm to 6pm) is when he's on the rooftop, so he can't go to the bakery then. 6. 6pm to 7pm: Driving to the water park with Susan. So that's option A. But during this time, he's driving, so he's not at the bakery. However, the bakery is closed after 7pm, so if he went to the bakery, it would have to be before 7pm. But during 6pm to 7pm, he's driving. So unless he went to the bakery before 6pm. Now, looking at the times, the only gap I can see is between 8am when he wakes up and 9am when Betty sees him.
